question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 61.00% on 2026-03-28 for the Metaculus question "Will Keiko Fujimori advance to the runoff in the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on Keiko Fujimori advancing to the 2026 Peruvian runoff will be strictly above 61% on March 28. The CP has dropped sharply from 67% to exactly 61% in one week (March 7-14), driven by polls showing Keiko's support declining while challengers like López Chau and Grozo rise. The CP is now exactly at the threshold, meaning even staying flat resolves NO — it must reverse direction for YES. While Keiko's fundamentals remain solid (2nd place in polls, 3/3 historical runoff track record, ~3-4pp lead over 3rd place), the downward momentum and rising challengers favor continued decline or stabilization below the threshold. The Grozo scandal could help Keiko but its impact is uncertain. I estimate ~40% probability the CP will be above 61% on March 28.

factors.description: Strong downward CP trend: 67% to 61% in one week (-6pp). Momentum favors continued decline below threshold.
CP is exactly AT the threshold (61.00%). Even staying flat resolves NO. Asymmetry: any continuation of trend = NO, needs reversal for YES.
Rising challengers (López Chau 6.8%, Grozo 4.3%) with upward trends could threaten Keiko's 2nd place, potentially driving CP down further.
Keiko has strong historical track record (3/3 runoffs in 2011, 2016, 2021) and currently holds 2nd place with ~3-4pp gap over 3rd. Fundamentals support CP around 55-65%.
Grozo scandal (Villaverde meetings) surfaced after latest poll was conducted, could hurt his rise and stabilize Keiko's positioning.
Recent March polls (Datum, IEP) likely already priced into the 61% CP level. Stabilization possible after initial adjustment.
High undecided voters (35%+) with 36 candidates creates genuine uncertainty about Keiko maintaining 2nd place, supporting continued downward CP revision.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.40 (40% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:

- **CP trend is sharply downward**: 67% → 61% in one week, a 6pp decline reflecting real polling developments showing Keiko's support weakening while challengers rise
- **Threshold asymmetry**: The CP sits exactly at 61.00%. It must go strictly above this — even staying flat means NO. This creates an inherent edge toward NO
- **Fundamentals still support Keiko**: She's in 2nd place with a 3-4pp gap over 3rd, and has made the runoff in all 3 previous elections. A rational CP somewhere in the 55-65% range is defensible
- **Wildcards could push either way**: The Grozo scandal could hurt a key challenger (helping Keiko/CP), but new polls could show continued fragmentation (hurting CP)
- **Net assessment**: The downward momentum and threshold positioning favor NO, but Keiko's solid fundamentals and potential stabilization prevent me from going much below 40%

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42139) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-28 09:46:43 is higher than 61.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 61.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-28 09:46:43, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
